skill.name: context-optimization
skill.description: Guides AI context optimization using compaction, observation masking, cache-friendly ordering, and context partitioning strategies.
skill.summary: Optimize long AI sessions by reducing token waste while preserving useful context.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static scanner reported external commands, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and a hardcoded URL, but review found only Markdown prose, front matter, and fenced Python pseudocode. No prompt injection, executable script, secret access, or network call is present in SKILL.md. The remaining concern is low residual risk from community-authored guidance with an external source link.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but these locations are fenced Python examples in documentation. They show context compaction, observation masking, and cache ordering pseudocode, not executable skill code or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are inside Markdown code fences and contain Python-like pseudocode. No Ruby backtick expression, command invocation, or execution path exists in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL Is Source Metadata
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is a front matter source link to the upstream repository. It is not used in a request, download, callback, or data exfiltration path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 4 is static metadata in the skill header. I found no evidence of network code or instructions to send user data to that URL.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches Plain Text
low_findings.description: The flagged high-severity crypto findings are documentation text about context masking, token usage, prompt stability, budget allocation, quality degradation, cache stability, and production guidance. No cryptographic algorithm, password hashing, key handling, or security protocol implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review shows the matched words appear in ordinary context-engineering guidance. There is no crypto API usage or recommendation to use weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Patterns Are Conceptual Guidance
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged system and network reconnaissance, but the locations discuss stable prompt content, storing verbose observations, and an evaluation-related skill link. These are context optimization concepts, not host or network discovery instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain no commands, endpoints, IP ranges, DNS lookups, or environment inspection. The wording is consistent with documentation-only optimization advice.

content.user_title: Optimize AI Context for Longer Tasks
content.value_statement: Long AI sessions can waste tokens and lose useful context. This skill guides compaction, masking, caching, and partitioning choices to preserve signal while reducing cost.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to compact long conversations or retrieved documents.
Defines observation masking rules for verbose tool outputs.
Shows cache-friendly ordering for stable prompts and reusable templates.
Describes context partitioning across focused sub-agents.
Provides budget triggers for reducing tokens, latency, and cost.
limitations: Provides guidance only; it does not run automated token analysis.
Does not include executable scripts, libraries, or integrations.
Requires users to measure quality impact in their own workflows.
Does not replace model-specific caching documentation or pricing details.

use_cases.title: Reduce Long Conversation Drift
use_cases.description: Use compaction rules to preserve decisions, constraints, and current goals when a chat approaches context limits.

use_cases.title: Design Production Agent Memory
use_cases.description: Apply observation masking and partitioning to keep agent traces smaller while retaining access to important facts.

use_cases.title: Lower Token Cost and Latency
use_cases.description: Reorder stable prompt sections and reuse templates to improve cache behavior for repeated workloads.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Context Waste
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a long prompt or conversation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this conversation for context waste. Identify verbose sections, repeated content, and old details that can be compacted safely.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Compaction Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning before a long task continues.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a compaction plan for this task. Separate decisions, open questions, current files, constraints, and details that can be summarized.

prompt_templates.title: Design Masking Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow design for tool-heavy agents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design observation masking rules for this agent workflow. Specify what to keep, what to summarize, what to replace with references, and when to restore details.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Agent Context Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert review of a production agent system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this agent architecture for context optimization. Recommend compaction triggers, cache-friendly ordering, partition boundaries, and quality checks.

output_examples.input: A long support-agent conversation with repeated tool logs and resolved questions.
output: Keep the current customer goal, active issue, latest decision, and unresolved blockers.
Summarize older tool logs into concise findings with references.
Remove repeated status text and resolved clarifications from active context.

output_examples.input: A production prompt that changes timestamps and request IDs near the beginning.
output_examples.output: Move stable instructions, tool definitions, and reusable templates first. Place timestamps, request IDs, and unique user data near the end to improve cache reuse.

output_examples.input: A research agent that collects many documents before writing a synthesis.
output_examples.output: Partition retrieval, evidence extraction, and synthesis into focused contexts. Aggregate only claims, citations, confidence notes, and unresolved conflicts.

best_practices: Measure token usage and quality before applying optimization changes.
Preserve current goals, constraints, decisions, and unresolved blockers during compaction.
Test optimization rules on realistic workloads before production use.
anti_patterns: Do not summarize system prompts or safety instructions away.
Do not mask recent observations that are still needed for active reasoning.
Do not optimize only for token count while ignoring answer quality.

faq.question: Does this skill increase the model context window?
faq.answer: No. It helps use the available context more efficiently through compaction, masking, caching, and partitioning.

faq.question: Can it automatically compact my conversation?
faq.answer: No. It provides methods and prompts that you can apply in Claude, Codex, Claude Code, or agent workflows.

faq.question: When should I use compaction?
faq.answer: Use compaction when context utilization is high, old details are less important, or response quality starts to degrade.

faq.question: What is observation masking?
faq.answer: Observation masking replaces verbose tool output with a compact reference after the important information has been extracted.

faq.question: How does cache-friendly ordering help?
faq.answer: Stable prompt sections placed first can improve cache reuse when a provider supports prefix or KV-cache optimization.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for sensitive data?
faq.answer: The skill does not transmit data. Users should still avoid placing secrets in prompts, summaries, or stored observation references.
